channel.title: Comments for page "The Picture of an Unian"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Picture of an Unian"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: me, very clever: it took me like, half of this article to realise that the "three-arrows-in-circle" was referring to the foundation logo and not the three arrows logo occasionally used by antifa

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my favorite things from the Secret Santa thread. (And it had a ton of great things in it!) Headcanon: humans are called "Unian" because some secure U.N. facilities were among the few surviving installations which could be studied by this alien civilization. Personal favorite part was where this painting is the aliens' first clear evidence that we do, indeed, have a torso.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Charmingly clever concept, with good execution to match. +1

item.title: Secret Santa 2017
item.content:encoded:
This is written for the Secret Santa 2017 Art Exchange, given to taylor_itkin who wanted something about scp-3864. Since he offered his own image in that article, a thought popped up: What if I make that the premises as a fanservice of sorts?

The rest is mixed in a setting where an unknown alien species looked at the works of Man (similar to how Shelley's Ozymandias did) and thinking that someone depicted in art has to be an important person.

Permission to be use the first image has been obtained by taylor_itkin himself, which is a self-photograph taken by himself.

Second image (URL link) originates from Wikimedia Commons user Grahampurse, and is licensed CC BY SA 4.0 (https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Rushmore_distant_view.jpg).

SCPs featured include:

  • SCP-3864 (I've Been Framed) by taylor_itkin
  • SCP-2722 (SCPS Solidarity) by Von Pincier

Frankly, the use of 2722-1 was me needing an excuse to have this unknown alien's writings appear as English somehow.
